name: pypandoc (Python Wrapper for Pandoc Document Converter)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Juho Vepsäläinen / Jessica Tegner

auth.notes: Local library; Pandoc binary must be installed or downloaded via pypandoc.download_pandoc()

performance.notes: Process spawn per conversion; Pandoc startup adds ~200ms per call

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for Pandoc — 40+ format conversion. import pypandoc. output = pypandoc.convert_text('# Hello', 'html', format='md'). pypandoc.convert_file('doc.docx', 'markdown'). pypandoc.download_pandoc() downloads platform binary. Extra args: extra_args=['--wrap=none', '--toc']. Filters: extra_args=['--filter', 'pandoc-citeproc']. Formats in: markdown, rst, docx, odt, html, latex, epub, mediawiki, org, etc. Formats out: html, pdf (via LaTeX), docx, odt, epub, rst, markdown, etc.

gotchas: Pandoc binary required — subprocess spawn per call (not for high-frequency use)
PDF output requires LaTeX distribution (TeX Live, MiKTeX) — large install
Format fidelity varies — complex DOCX features may not translate perfectly
best_when: One-time or batch document format conversion (DOCX → Markdown, RST → HTML)
Documentation pipelines converting between markup languages
When 40+ format support is needed from a single library
avoid_when: High-frequency realtime conversion (subprocess overhead per call)
Simple Markdown-to-HTML (use mistune or markdown-it-py instead)
Environments where Pandoc binary cannot be installed
summary: pypandoc wraps Pandoc for 40+ format document conversion. convert_text('# Hello', 'html', format='md'). convert_file('doc.docx', 'markdown'). pypandoc.download_pandoc() installs Pandoc binary. MIT. Process spawn per call — not for high-frequency use. PDF requires LaTeX. Best for batch document conversion pipelines (DOCX↔Markdown↔RST↔HTML↔EPUB). For simple Markdown-to-HTML use mistune instead.
